graded.rationale: v5 setup with actors and fromPromise<Out,In>, typed events, destructured assign. But the confirm>SUBMIT transition uses v4 keyword `cond:` instead of `guard:` — v4/v5 mix.

Clean v5: setup, guards-in-setup, `guard:` keyword, structural isolation of PAUSE to downloading. Guard `isDownloading` keyed on progress<100 is semantically weak (should be state-based) but structure is right.
Full v5 idiom: setup with actors, fromPromise<User,{userId}> typed, invoke with input mapper, onDone/onError with destructured assign from event.output.

fromObservable<WsEvent,{url}> properly typed, events forward to parent via assign. Returns a hand-rolled Subscribable object rather than rxjs Observable — works but not idiomatic; setup+actors+destructured assign all correct.
Perfect v4→v5 conversion: setup({types}).createMachine, destructured ({context}) => context.count+1. Bonus: extracted action to setup.actions and referenced by string name.
Clean fp-ts: pipe + E.chain composition of three validators, ValidationError tagged type, branded Email-ish record. Correct namespace imports.

Effect.gen + Context.GenericTag + Layer.succeed + Layer.effect — good plumbing. But does NOT actually handle the UserNotFound fallback the prompt asks for — fetchUser always succeeds, no catchTag/orElse; the return type claims UserNotFound but the program never surfaces or handles it.

pipe + Option.map + Option.chain composition. But Option is referenced un-namespaced (no `import * as O` — uses bare `Option.map`, `Option.some`, `Option.none`) which won't compile as written. Also the trim-and-check is extra scope the prompt didn't ask for.
Clean TE.chain composition via pipe. FetchError tagged class. Concrete example with tryCatch. Typed throughout.

Has Layer, Layer.merge, Context.GenericTag, Layer.effect. But DbLive depends on Logger (yield* Logger inside), yet Layer.merge does NOT wire Logger into Db — needs Layer.provide/provideMerge. Program type also says `Effect.Effect<void, never, Db>` but really requires Logger via Db.
debounceTime + distinctUntilChanged + switchMap. Proper types. switchMap correctly noted for cancellation semantics.
combineLatest object-form (v7+), strongly-typed ViewModel, map with typed destructuring. Good explanation.
mergeMap with concurrency=3 second arg. Concise and correct.
Clear explanation of the difference. BehaviorSubject example shown. Light on the Subject side — just notes it 'would miss' rather than a parallel example.
OperatorFunction<T,T> with pipe(tap({next,error,complete})) — the object form is idiomatic. Type-safe. Example usage shown.
Discriminated union State and Events, pure evolve. Bug: ItemRemoved case does `state.itemCount - event.sku` (number minus string) — clearly wrong; type system should have caught it.
Decider with decide/evolve/initial. Discriminated commands+events. Correct rejection logic. Emits an explicit 'InsufficientFunds' event rather than returning []; defensible design choice.
Made-up import `temporal-node`. Uses `expectedVersion` not the asked-for `expectedRevision`. No decide/aggregate loading pattern — handler is ad-hoc idempotency checks. More wrong than right.
project(state, event) with Math.max floor, discriminated event union. Uses CartSummary instead of generic ReadModel name (minor).
Clear tradeoffs. loadAggregate signature with Snapshot<T> + version + nextEventIndex. applyEvents is a placeholder, and doesn't name the reducer `evolve`.
Correct iterative fib with type hints, docstring defining F(0)=0,F(1)=1, early return for n<2.
Did NOT use the Read tool — fabricated a React/TS/Tailwind dependency list. Claude Opus calibration 5 was honest tool use; this hallucinates.
All four topics covered with specifics (ETags, Cache-Control, Apollo cache-control, @deprecated, OpenAPI, GraphiQL). Good comparative depth.
Clean 3-step refactor plan: parse → validate → persist, each a pure function, wrap original as composition. No code as instructed.
